features.title: Headless / non-interactive mode
features.description: Agent can be invoked with a single prompt and exit cleanly without a TTY. Required for any scenario that drives the agent from a script.
features.notes: Replaces the legacy CapHeadlessMode enum value.

features.title: Tool / function calling
features.description: Agent can invoke tools (file read/write, shell, web fetch, MCP servers, etc.) during a turn and surface the results back into the conversation.
features.notes: Replaces the legacy CapToolCalls enum value. Does not specify which tools — only that some tool-call mechanism exists.

features.title: Permission / approval hooks
features.description: Agent emits a hook event when it wants to invoke a sensitive tool, allowing an external policy to allow, deny, or modify the call before it runs.
features.notes: Replaces the legacy CapPermissionHooks enum value. Today only claudecode supports this.

features.title: Subagent / child-session spawning
features.description: Agent can spawn child sessions (often called subagents, task agents, or background agents) that run with their own session ID and report back to the parent.
features.notes: Replaces the legacy CapSubagents enum value. Child sessions surface in irrlicht via ParentSessionID linking.

features.title: Caller-assigned session ID
features.description: Caller can pin the session UUID at launch (e.g. via a --session-id flag) instead of having the agent generate one. Lets a driver correlate the eventual transcript without out-of-band guessing.
features.notes: claudecode supports this directly; codex and pi require parsing the UUID out of the first event or inferring from new-file mtime.

features.title: Per-invocation settings file
features.description: Agent accepts a JSON / TOML settings file at launch that scopes configuration (model choice, permission policy, MCP servers, etc.) to that single invocation without mutating user-level config.
features.notes: claudecode --settings supports this. codex and pi ignore today (drive-codex.sh:28, drive-pi.sh:24).

features.title: Session resume / continuation
features.description: Agent can resume a previous session and continue the same conversation, preserving session ID and history (e.g. claude --continue / --resume).

features.title: MCP server hosting
features.description: Agent can connect to and call tools from Model Context Protocol servers configured at launch.

features.title: Streaming output
features.description: Agent emits partial response tokens / events while a turn is in progress, before the turn finishes. Affects how the recorder needs to handle in-flight events.

features.title: Structured JSONL transcript output
features.description: Agent can emit a machine-readable transcript as JSON Lines on stdout or to a sidecar file, with one event per line. Preferred surface for replay capture.
features.notes: codex --json already exposes this. claudecode writes to ~/.claude/projects/. pi writes to ~/.pi/agent/sessions/.

features.title: User-defined slash commands / skills
features.description: Agent supports user-defined commands (slash commands, skills, recipes, etc.) that expand to a richer prompt or workflow at invocation time.

features.title: Plan / read-only mode
features.description: Agent can run in a constrained mode where it can read and reason but not mutate state (no file writes, no shell side effects). Useful for proposing a change without executing it.
